To: Northwind Airlines Customer Care
From: Jane Doe (jane.doe@email.com)
Booking reference: QX7T2P
Flight: NW482, London Heathrow (LHR) → Athens (ATH), 14 March 2026

Subject: Claim for compensation and expenses under UK261 — Flight NW482 cancellation

Dear Northwind Airlines,

I am writing to claim statutory compensation and reimbursement of expenses following the cancellation of flight NW482 (LHR→ATH), scheduled to depart on 14 March 2026 at 18:40.

You notified me of the cancellation by email on the morning of 14 March 2026 — the same day as departure, well within 14 days. You rebooked me onto flight NW488 arriving Athens at 13:10 on 15 March 2026, approximately 16 hours later than my original scheduled arrival.

1. Compensation. Under Regulation (EC) No 261/2004 as retained in UK law, a cancellation notified fewer than 14 days before departure entitles me to fixed compensation of £350 for this flight (distance 1,500–3,500 km), given the rebooked arrival delay of more than three hours. Your email attributes the cancellation to 'an unexpected technical fault with the aircraft.' Established case law (Wallentin-Hermann v Alitalia, C-549/07; van der Lans v KLM, C-257/14) confirms that technical faults inherent in the normal operation of an aircraft are NOT 'extraordinary circumstances' and therefore do not exempt the airline from paying compensation. I accordingly claim £350.

2. Expenses (duty of care). Under Article 9, you owe me accommodation, meals and transport during the overnight disruption, and your email expressly invited me to retain receipts. I incurred the following, evidenced by the attached Heathrow Plaza Hotel receipt (paid on card ending 4471):
   - Overnight room: £128.00
   - Dinner: £22.50
   - Breakfast: £14.00
   - Airport transfer (taxi): £18.00
   Total expenses: £182.50

Total amount claimed: £532.50 (£350 compensation + £182.50 expenses).

Please pay this sum within 14 days by bank transfer or to my original payment method. If you decline the compensation on 'extraordinary circumstances' grounds, please provide full documentary evidence of the technical fault and why it could not have been avoided. Absent such proof, I will escalate to the relevant ADR body or the UK Civil Aviation Authority, and reserve the right to pursue the matter in the County Court.

Attachments: cancellation email (14 March 2026); Heathrow Plaza Hotel receipt (HPX-559102).

Yours faithfully,
Jane Doe

Subject: UK261 Compensation and Expense Reimbursement Claim — Flight NW482, Booking QX7T2P

Dear Northwind Airlines,

I am writing to claim compensation and reimbursement following the cancellation of flight NW482 (London Heathrow LHR → Athens ATH), scheduled to depart on 14 March 2026 at 18:40, under booking reference QX7T2P.

You cancelled this flight and notified me on the day of departure, rebooking me onto flight NW488 the following morning (15 March 2026), which arrived in Athens at approximately 13:10 — an arrival delay of around 16 hours versus my original itinerary.

1. Statutory compensation — £350
Under UK Regulation (EC) No 261/2004, Articles 5 and 7, I am entitled to fixed compensation of £350. The flight distance (London Heathrow to Athens, approximately 2,400 km) falls within the 1,500–3,500 km band; the cancellation was notified less than 14 days before departure; and my rebooked arrival was delayed by well over three hours.

Your email attributes the cancellation to 'an unexpected technical fault with the aircraft.' Technical and mechanical faults are inherent to the normal exercise of an air carrier's activity and do not constitute 'extraordinary circumstances' under the Regulation (see CJEU C-549/07, Wallentin-Hermann). Compensation is therefore due.

2. Duty of care — £182.50
Under Article 9, you owed me a right to care during the overnight disruption. As instructed in your email, I arranged my own accommodation and meals and retained the receipt.
